issues: OCR is heavily fragmented and garbled — words from adjacent columns are bleeding in (e.g. '2-1', 'Tier 2', 'fourth', 'Dave ingbull' which may be 'Dave Tomlinson' or 'Mike Claringbull'). The full article text is not recoverable from this OCR.
The headline 'Sockeyes Win in Overtime' does not appear in the OCR — it was likely AI-invented rather than extracted from the scan.

The date April 23 1987 is noted as the game day in the draft, but the Centennial Cup final (5-2 over Humboldt) took place on May 9 1987 — an April 23 Sockeyes vs Humboldt Broncos game would be outside the Centennial Cup tournament timeline, raising questions about what round/tournament this game belongs to.
The score 4-3 OT and scorer Tony Bobbitt at 1:04 appear plausible from the OCR fragment, but surrounding context is too garbled to confirm all details with confidence.
review.notes: OCR is too fragmented to verify the draft with confidence. The core facts (Tony Bobbitt OT goal at 1:04, 4-3 win over Humboldt Broncos) appear to be supported by the OCR fragment, but adjacent-column noise is bleeding heavily into the text (e.g. 'Tier 2', 'fourth', 'Dave ingbull'). The headline was not found in the OCR and appears AI-invented. Additionally, April 23 1987 is before the Centennial Cup tournament (May 1987), so this may be a different round or a pre-tournament game — the draft should clarify this or an editor should confirm the tournament context from the original image. Byline appears to be a Canadian Press (CP) wire dispatch with no named reporter.
